_comment: Absolute performance budgets — the single source of truth for all three perf scopes (DB-layer runner, k6 HTTP, BenchmarkDotNet). A scope exits non-zero when a measured value breaches its budget. Most budgets are calibrated from a 1M-row run on a dev machine (2026-06); RECALIBRATE on your target/CI hardware (set each ~20-30% above the observed p95/mean). A missing key = measure but never fail.
_comment_stats: statsSummary/statsLatencyPercentiles/statsTokenUsage/statsModelBreakdown/statsCostEstimate/statsCallTrends and httpP95Ms.statisticsDashboard are set to TARGET values, NOT to current measurements: these aggregations client-evaluate at scale (issue #246) and currently measure ~3.7-4.4s (~5-6s over HTTP), so the suite is INTENTIONALLY RED on them. The raw SQL aggregate is <1ms, so a few hundred ms is achievable once #246 lands. Keeping them red makes the suite an active reminder. When #246 is fixed, re-run `perf/run.sh --scopes db-layer,http` and confirm these go green at the target; loosen only if the measured value is legitimately and unavoidably higher.
